{{Quote|Nobody...nobody...calls me 'cooky.'|Brass Marshoo|<ref name="TDoCT" />}}
'''Brass Marshoo''' was a [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Chef/Legends|chef]] who lived in [[Otoh Gunga/Legends|Otoh Gunga]] on the planet [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] during the rise of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Biography==
Marshoo was named for a [[Marshoo|fish species]] of Naboo. He once served in the [[Gungan Grand Army/Legends|Gungan Grand Army]] as a [[General/Legends|general]].

[[File:MarshooGCW.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Brass Marshoo at the Lake Retreat during the Galactic Civil War]]
Prior to the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]], Brass Marshoo owned a restaurant called [[General Marshoo's Eatery]] at [[Otoh Gunga/Legends|Otoh Gunga]]. In [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]], he discovered that the Gungan [[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]] attempted to steal his food supplies. The chef reacted violently but [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Roos Tarpals/Legends|Roos Tarpals]] arrived to settle the dispute. Tarpals arrested Binks for his theft but Marshoo did not believe that the captain would really punish his friend Jar Jar. The chef claimed that Tarpals used his status to protect Binks and became particularly enraged, interfering with Tarpals' arrest of Binks. In his anger, he knocked Tarpals unconscious to get at Binks. Binks, with help from Tarpals' [[Kaadu/Legends|kaadu]] managed to fend off Marshoo. After regaining consciousness, Tarpals tied Marshoo up and then arrested Binks.<ref name="TDoCT" />

Three decades later, during the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Brass Marshoo still lived on Naboo.<ref name="SWG:BM">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=static|Brass Marshoo|Naboo}}</ref> In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="SWG_timeline">{{SWG_timeline}}</ref> Marshoo was present at the [[Varykino/Legends|Lake Retreat]] where he hired a [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] to kill some wild animals, including [[fanned rawl]]s, [[Peko-peko/Legends|peko-pekos]] and [[Mott/Legends|motts]].<ref name="SWG:BM" />

==Behind the scenes==
Brass Marshoo first appeared in ''[[The Death of Captain Tarpals]]'', a short [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] featured in [[Star Wars Tales 3|''Star Wars Tales'' #3]].<ref name="TDoCT" />

Brass Marshoo also appeared as a [[Wikipedia:Non-player character|non-player character]] in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|massively multiplayer online roleplaying game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG:BM" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> Marshoo was located at the Lake Retreat where he gave three completable missions. However, these quests were bugged and lacked any text dialogue strings, rendering it impossible to have derived much context from them.<ref name="SWG:BM" /><ref name="NabooMap">{{SonyForumsCite|url=swg/board/message?board.id=game_guides&message.id=62324.htm|text=List of Static Mission NPC's for Naboo and Rori}} {{C|"username/password: swganh"}}</ref> For this reason, these quests were deactivated by the developers relatively early in the game's lifespan. Brass Marshoo remained at the Lake Retreat, but would only tell players who interacted with him that he did not have time for them.
